verbGlossary.FUNDED BY: Owner or lender {target} that provides the capital backing a budget or payment
verbGlossary.MANAGED BY: {source} responsible for day-to-day administration and control of {target}
verbGlossary.FINANCED BY: Lender providing construction loan or credit facility against the project budget
verbGlossary.OVERSEEN BY: {source} providing program-level governance over budget or forecast
verbGlossary.ALLOCATED BY: {source} that distributes budget dollars across line items and cost codes
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED TO: {source} whose scope of work a budget line item is earmarked for
verbGlossary.DEFINED BY: {source} that establishes the cost code structure for the project
verbGlossary.CHARGED TO: {source} whose work activity causes costs to post against a cost code
verbGlossary.STRUCTURED BY: {source} that defines the WBS hierarchy for the project
verbGlossary.REPORTED TO: {source} that receives financial reports, forecasts, or variance summaries
verbGlossary.CONTRACTED TO: {source} awarded a commitment (subcontract or service agreement) for defined scope
verbGlossary.ISSUED BY: {source} that creates and sends a commitment or purchase order to {target}
verbGlossary.BONDED BY: Surety {target} that provides a performance or payment bond securing a commitment
verbGlossary.INSURED BY: {source}carrier that provides liability or builders risk coverage for a commitment
verbGlossary.AWARDED TO: {source} selected to fulfill a purchase order, change order, or bid scope
verbGlossary.SCOPED TO: {source} whose trade scope is defined by a specific commitment line item
verbGlossary.PRICED BY: {source} that provides cost pricing on a commitment line item or change event
verbGlossary.INITIATED BY: {source} that creates a change event to document a scope or cost change
verbGlossary.CAUSED BY: Design or engineering {target} whose design decision or error originated the change event
verbGlossary.DIRECTED BY: Owner {target} that directs a scope change via owner directive
verbGlossary.TRIGGERED BY: Regulatory {target} whose requirement or code interpretation triggered a change event
verbGlossary.QUOTED BY: {source} that provides a line-item price quote on a proposed change
verbGlossary.REVIEWED BY: {source} that evaluates {target} for accuracy or approval
verbGlossary.SUBMITTED BY: {source} that formally submits an invoice, pay application, or change order request
verbGlossary.APPROVED BY: {source} with {target} to approve a change order or budget modification
verbGlossary.AUTHORIZED BY: {source} that grants formal authorization to proceed with a financial action
verbGlossary.PERFORMED BY: {source} that executes the work described in a CCO line item
verbGlossary.EXECUTED BY: {source} that carries out a budget modification or transfer
verbGlossary.INCURRED BY: {source} that bears a direct cost on its own account (not through a commitment)
verbGlossary.BILLED BY: {source} that sends a bill or invoice for testing, monitoring, or other services
verbGlossary.INVOICED BY: {source} that issues a formal invoice for a direct cost line item
verbGlossary.PAID BY: Owner or lender {target} that makes payment on a pay application or invoice
verbGlossary.DRAW AGAINST: Lender {target} against whose construction loan an owner invoice is drawn
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED BY: {target} or CM that certifies a pay application or schedule of values for payment
verbGlossary.WITHHELD FROM: {source} from whose payment a retainage percentage is held back
verbGlossary.HELD BY: {source} that holds retainage funds in trust until release conditions are met
verbGlossary.RELEASED BY: Owner {target} that authorizes release of retainage upon substantial completion
verbGlossary.DISBURSED BY: {source} that distributes payment funds to downstream parties
verbGlossary.PAID TO: {source} that receives payment for completed work, materials, or utility services
verbGlossary.SIGNED BY: {source} that executes a lien waiver to release lien rights upon payment
verbGlossary.COLLECTED BY: {target} that gathers lien waivers from all subcontractors and suppliers each pay period
verbGlossary.PREPARED BY: {source} that creates a schedule of values, forecast, or cost projection
verbGlossary.BROKEN DOWN BY: {source} that itemizes its contract value into schedule of values line items
verbGlossary.ESTABLISHED BY: {target} that sets allowance amounts in the contract for undefined scope items
verbGlossary.SPECIFIED BY: {source}that defines allowance scope in contract documents
verbGlossary.MONITORED BY: {source} that tracks and reviews {target} without direct control
verbGlossary.PROJECTED BY: {source} that creates cash flow projections for draw scheduling
verbGlossary.ATTRIBUTED TO: {source} whose actions or scope are the source of a variance or cost growth
verbGlossary.CALCULATED BY: {source} that computes earned value or forecast performance metrics
verbGlossary.COMMITTED TO: {source} to which cost dollars are contractually committed via subcontracts or POs
verbGlossary.TRACKED BY: {source} that monitors a financial metric over time for trend analysis
verbGlossary.MEASURED BY: {source} that calculates and reports a financial performance metric
verbGlossary.LEVIED BY: Regulatory {target} that imposes fines or penalties recorded as direct costs
verbGlossary.RETAINED BY: {source} profit dollars that remain with {target} after all project costs are paid
verbGlossary.SET BY: {source} establishes the markup percentage or fee structure applied by {target}
verbGlossary.NEGOTIATED WITH: {source} fee or markup is commercially negotiated with {target} during contract formation
verbGlossary.MEASURED ACROSS: {source} metric is calculated across the portfolio of projects with {target}
verbGlossary.MEASURED AGAINST: {source} collection metric is measured against {target}'s payment behavior
verbGlossary.UNDERWRITTEN BY: {source} capacity is assessed and guaranteed by {target} surety company
